interaction_phrase: together with Hyperion Research, we published a report
occurrence_sentence: This is why, together with Hyperion Research, we published a report to explore what's next: early fault-tolerant quantum computers (eFTQC).

interaction_phrase: adopted new waves of technology, from vector processors to GPUs, parallel computing and TPUs, to build ever larger and more capable supercomputers
occurrence_sentence: The HPC community has long adopted new waves of technology, from vector processors to GPUs, parallel computing and TPUs, to build ever larger and more capable supercomputers.
This is why, together with Hyperion Research, we published a report to explore what's next: early fault-tolerant quantum computers (eFTQC).

eFTQC QPUs will not make large-scale data analysis or near-term AI training faster, and algorithms like Grover's search, which provide only quadratic speedups, are unlikely to offset the slower operation speeds of quantum hardware. eFTQC will be a powerful tool for the HPC community, but its strengths are domain-specific and need to be well understood, which involves co-designing applications with HPC specialists, domain experts and quantum hardware vendors.

occurrence_sentence: At major U.S. DOE facilities such as Los Alamos, NERSC, and other leadership computing centers, these fields represent between 13 and 54 percent of current computational workloads.
target_actor: National Energy Research Scientific Computing Center (NERSC)

Boson 4 was launched on Google Cloud for everyone to access and test earlier this year, marking an important product milestone.

On Boson 4, our single-qubit device, we set a world record for bit-flip protection , 7 minutes, far beyond the tens of millisecond of other superconducting quantum systems. [Boson 4](https://alice-bob.com/products/quantum-cloud-felis/boson-4/) was launched on Google Cloud for everyone to access and test earlier this year, marking an important product milestone.

For example, we've shown that a quantum computer using cat qubits could run Shor's algorithm with up to [60 times fewer qubits than other approaches](https://doi.org/10.1103/PhysRevLett.131.040602), and 200 times fewer when employing [advanced error correction schemes](https://arxiv.org/abs/2401.09541).

The whitepaper is designed to help anyone, regardless of their background, grasp the most crucial aspects of quantum computing, as well as understand the cat qubit and why hardware efficiency is so critical.
To make it even more accessible, the full whitepaper is available both in a fully illustrated, printable version and as an audiobook narrated by Christopher Bishop.

Alice & Bob, a global frontrunner in the race for fault-tolerant quantum computing, today announced a raise of €100 million in its Series B funding round, led by Future French Champions (FFC), AVP (AXA Venture Partners) and Bpifrance.

In investing in Alice & Bob, Future French Champions recognizes the company as a French leader in quantum computing.
AVP is therefore proud to support the company in their mission to reduce the hardware requirements for building a practical, large-scale quantum computer.
We are thrilled to once again contribute to the groundbreaking work that Alice & Bob is doing to scale quantum computers for real-world applications.
Since Alice & Bob's inception, Elaia Partners has been proud to stand behind the company as it strives to achieve energy, and hardware-efficient fault-tolerant quantum computers using cat qubits.

occurrence_sentence: For this next phase, we are expanding. We have built production infrastructure, on a pre-industrial scale. What does that look like you may ask? In this new facility, we can house not three, but twenty dilution refrigerators.

March Alice & Bob is born Nature Physics publishes Raphaël Lescanne's work on cat qubits, a new type of quantum bit inherently protected from a whole class of errors: "bit-flips".
Raphaël's fellow PhD student, Théau, convinces him to start Alice & Bob and, together, to bring cat qubits from the lab to industry.
